HomePage.title: Your adventure begins here.
HomePage.subtitle: Guided tours in the heart of Peneda-Gerês National Park.
HomePage.regionTeaserSubtitle: The Terras de Barroso sit at the heart of Peneda-Gerês National Park. A granite highland plateau dotted with medieval villages, wild rivers and one of the last Iberian wolf packs on the Peninsula.
HomePage.howStep1Body: Every programme starts with a conversation. We don't sell seats on a bus — we design days around what you actually want to do, eat, and feel.
HomePage.howStep2Body: Your guide grew up in these valleys. Your host has been making wine in this quinta for thirty years. Local knowledge isn't a feature — it's the whole point.
HomePage.howStep3Body: Transfers, accommodation, table reservations, permits, weather contingencies. We think of it so you don't have to. You bring curiosity — we carry everything else.
HomePage.whatsappCtaBody: No forms to fill in. Message or call us — we reply within the hour and are happy to build a custom experience around you.
HomePage.whatsappCtaWhatsappDesc: The quickest way to reach us. We reply within the hour and love planning custom itineraries over chat.
HomePage.whatsappCtaCallDesc: Speak directly with one of our guides. Available during daylight hours, seven days a week.

TourDetail.difficultyFullModerate: This tour is rated moderate. We recommend regular walking. May include ascents and rocky sections.
TourDetail.difficultyFullChallenging: This tour is rated challenging. For participants with good fitness and mountain hiking experience.
TourDetail.difficultyFullExpert: This tour is rated expert. Advanced level. Requires excellent fitness and technical mountain experience.

AboutPage.title: Local guides. Local Knowledge.
AboutPage.subtitle: We are a small, local team passionate about the territory we call home. Every tour is guided by someone who grew up here.
AboutPage.metaDescription: Meet the local guides of Natur Barroso — the team that lives and breathes Barroso.
AboutPage.storyP1: Natur Barroso was born from a simple conviction: Barroso is one of the most extraordinary territories in Europe, and it deserved someone to present it to the world with honesty and passion. Not as a generic tourism product, but as what it truly is — a granite plateau of silence, where nature and human culture have coexisted for millennia.
AboutPage.storyP2: Our guides are not just trained professionals — they are people who grew up in this territory, who know its secrets and genuinely believe that a morning in Barroso can change the way someone sees the world.
AboutPage.storyP3: We operate in small groups because we believe the quality of experience depends on individual attention. We work with local producers, family-run accommodation and village restaurants because we believe tourism should benefit the communities that make it possible.
AboutPage.value1Body: We are from the region and know every trail, village and story. Nothing is generic.
AboutPage.value2Body: Maximum 8–12 people per tour. Individual attention, group pace, personalised experience.
AboutPage.value3Body: We collaborate with local producers, accommodation and restaurants. The tourism we practice stays in the region.

RegionPage.title: Discover the North
RegionPage.subtitle: A high granite plateau dotted with medieval villages, and wild rivers.
RegionPage.metaDescription: Discover the Terras de Barroso — waterfalls, medieval villages, castles and Peneda-Gerês National Park.
RegionPage.season1Note: Waterfalls in full flow. Wild flowers. Ideal for hiking.
RegionPage.season2Note: Swimming in waterfalls and rivers. Long days. Book in advance.
RegionPage.season3Note: Best for wildlife and photography. Fewer tourists. Chestnuts and harvest season.
RegionPage.season4Note: Snow-covered plateau. Maximum water flows. Special 4×4 expeditions.
RegionPage.why1Body: One of Portugal's last Iberian wolf packs. Roe deer, wild boar and over 200 bird species.
RegionPage.why2Body: Medieval castles, ruined monasteries and granite villages where time still rules.
RegionPage.why3Body: Vinho do Douro, Barrosã beef, heather honey, goat cheese. The home of a world renowned cuisine.
